briefing: Factory achieves $1.5B valuation to develop AI coding solutions for enterprises.

articles.title: Factory hits $1.5B valuation to build AI coding for enterprises
articles.snippet: The three-year-old startup raised $150 million led by Khosla Ventures.
articles.ai_summary: Factory, a startup focused on AI coding for enterprises, has achieved a $1.5 billion valuation after raising $150 million led by Khosla Ventures. This funding will help accelerate its growth in the competitive AI market.
articles.why_it_matters: Highlights the growing investment and interest in AI coding solutions for enterprises.

articles.title: Anthropic won't own MCP 'design flaw' putting 200K servers at risk, researcher says
articles.snippet: Bug or feature? A design flaw – or expected behavior based on a bad design choice, depending on who is telling the story – baked into Anthropic's official Model Context Protocol (MCP) puts as many as 200,000 servers at risk of complete takeover, according to security researchers.…
articles.ai_summary: A design flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol (MCP) may expose 200,000 servers to security risks, raising concerns among researchers about the implications for data security. The distinction between a flaw and expected behavior complicates accountability.
articles.why_it_matters: Raises critical security concerns for AI systems and their infrastructure.

articles.title: I tried the new Gemini app for Mac - and it's better than the website in one big way

articles.title: NodeWeaver says its perpetual licensing beats VMware’s perpetual price hikes
articles.snippet: 'I think you can run this thing on a potato,' NodeWeaver CTO Alan Conboy said. Broadcom's price increases and policy changes have led many VMware customers to look for other options. Nodeweaver is positioning itself as an alternative for customers running computing workloads in far-flung edge locations, from cruise ships to solar farms in Sub-Saharan Africa, and it is taking cost out of the hardware needed as well.…

articles.title: MixAtlas: Uncertainty-aware Data Mixture Optimization for Multimodal LLM Midtraining
articles.snippet:
This paper was accepted at the Workshop on Navigating and Addressing Data Problems for Foundation Models (NADPFM) at ICLR 2026.
Principled domain reweighting can substantially improve sample efficiency and downstream generalization; however, data-mixture optimization for multimodal pretraining remains underexplored. Current multimodal training recipes tune mixtures from only a single perspective such as data format or task type. We introduce MixAtlas, a principled framework for compute-efficient
articles.ai_summary: The paper discusses MixAtlas, a new framework for optimizing data mixtures in multimodal training, which could enhance sample efficiency and generalization. This research addresses a gap in current multimodal pretraining methods.
articles.why_it_matters: Advances multimodal AI training methodologies for better performance.
